algorithms.title: Adult 2-Rescuer BLS Sequence
algorithms.description: Coordinated multi-rescuer adult CPR, from role assignment through advanced-airway ventilation.
steps: Confirm arrest and assign roles: compressor, airway/ventilation, AED operator, and team leader.
Rescuer 1 delivers compressions at 100–120/min, depth 2–2.4 inches, with full recoil.
Rescuer 2 manages the airway and gives 2 breaths during the brief pause after every 30 compressions (30:2).
Operate the AED as soon as it arrives; minimize pre-shock and post-shock pauses; resume compressions immediately after any shock.
Switch the compressor about every 2 minutes (5 cycles), ideally during AED analysis, completing the change in under 10 seconds.
When an advanced airway is placed, switch to continuous compressions with 1 breath every 6 seconds (~10/min).
Use closed-loop communication and reassess throughout; continue until ROSC or handoff to advanced care.

algorithms.title: Elements of Effective Resuscitation Team Dynamics
algorithms.description: The behaviors that make a resuscitation team perform reliably under pressure.
steps: Clear roles and responsibilities: everyone knows their job.
Closed-loop communication: orders are repeated back and completion confirmed.
Clear messages: concise, specific, and directed to a named person.
Knowledge sharing: information and concerns are voiced to the team.
Constructive intervention: members respectfully raise safety issues in real time.
Reassessment and summarizing: the leader re-evaluates and restates the plan.
Mutual respect: professional, supportive tone regardless of hierarchy.
